cs111: CS 111 is a 3-credit course dealing with an introduction to computer science. The course is taught primarily in Python but will cover complex topics such as iteration, recursion, and interpreters.
cs180: CS 180 is a 3-credit course with an online section and some in-person sections. Students learn the fundamentals of data science and machine learning through programming in python.
cs235: CS 235 is a 3-credit course with an online section and some in-person sections. Students learn the data structures and how to implement them in C++.
cs260: CS 260 is a 3-credit course with one online section and one in-person section. Students learn the fundamentals of web programming through HTML, CSS, Javastrap, React, and other related technologies.
math290: Math 290 is a 3-credit course to prepare students for transitioning into advanced mathematics. Students learn all about proofs!
acc200: Acc 200 is a 3-credit course that provides an introduction to financial accounting.
acc241: Acc 241 is a 3-credit course that covers the legal environment of business.
acc300: Acc 300 is a 3-credit course that builds on the principles of financial accounting.
acc301: Acc 301 is a 3-credit course that continues the study of financial accounting.
acc310: Acc 310 is a 3-credit course that focuses on the use of accounting information for internal decision-making.
acc329: Acc 329 is a 3-credit course that covers the design and use of accounting information systems.
acc401: Acc 401 is a 3-credit course that covers advanced topics in financial accounting.
acc403: Acc 403 is a 3-credit course that covers the principles of federal income taxation.
acc405: Acc 405 is a 3-credit course that covers the principles and procedures of auditing.
acc406: Acc 406 is a 3-credit course that covers accounting for international operations.
